candidate_summary: Paired-form curriculum LLM pretraining inspired by Aikido kata: progressive-difficulty kata-stages, per-stage mastery-checkpoint gate before advancement, paired uke-tori bilateral training (both initiator and responder sides), and failure-driven adaptive sample resampling.

results.name: Prompt Curriculum Learning Efficient LLM Post-Training (2510.01135)
results.description: PCL selects intermediate-difficulty prompts via learned value model — curriculum-driven post-training.
results.rationale: Difficulty-selected curriculum prompts overlap candidate's progressive-difficulty staging concept, but PCL targets post-training prompt selection without mastery-gate checkpoints, paired bilateral training, or stage replay. Same family (difficulty curriculum) but different artefact (post-training prompt selection vs. pretraining staged mastery gates).

results.name: Limits Curriculum Learning Post-Training LLM (OpenReview)
results.description: CL as mechanism to exploit difficulty during post-training; mixed results across SFT and RL.
results.rationale: Examines CL difficulty exploitation in post-training. Same difficulty-curriculum family but no checkpoint gate, no bilateral paired training, no resample-on-failure. Partial paradigm overlap.

results.name: Training LLM Agents RL Gyms Curriculum Design (Turing)
results.description: RL gyms benefit from performance-based progression with advancement criteria + regression to earlier stages on threshold drop.
results.rationale: Performance-based progression with advancement criteria + regression on threshold drop is functionally the mastery-gate-checkpoint + failure-driven stage-replay mechanism. Strong functional twin to gating/replay aspect; lacks paired uke-tori bilateral training, but covers the gate-checkpoint effect.

results.name: Curriculum Learning LLM Pretraining Learning Dynamics (2601.21698)
results.description: 200+ models up to 100B tokens with curriculum stages + pacing + interleaved variants.
results.rationale: Systematic curriculum-stage LLM pretraining with pacing and interleaved variants. Covers staged-curriculum pretraining paradigm but does not introduce mastery-checkpoint gates or paired bilateral training. The candidate's own novelty section explicitly carves itself off against this paper.

results.name: Beyond Random Sampling Efficient LM Pretraining Curriculum (2506.11300)
results.rationale: Curriculum-order data scheduling for pretraining — overlaps difficulty-order aspect of kata stages but no gating, no paired bilateral, no replay. Candidate's novelty section explicitly differentiates from this paper.

results.name: Curriculum-Guided Layer Scaling LM Pretraining (2506.11389)
results.description: Stratified domain/complexity curriculum + per-stage layer scaling.
results.rationale: Per-stage stratified curriculum LM pretraining. Same staged curriculum family, but per-stage scaling is a model architecture intervention rather than mastery-gate checkpoint. Differentiated by candidate.

results.name: LR Decay Curriculum-Based LLM Pretraining (2511.18903)
results.description: Schedule interaction between learning-rate decay + curriculum order; staged-pretraining best-practice.
results.rationale: LR-decay-curriculum interaction for staged pretraining. Covers staged-curriculum + schedule coupling but no checkpoint mastery gate or paired bilateral. Candidate differentiates explicitly.

results.name: Bilateral Training Curriculum LLM 2026 Feb (2602.12036)
results.description: Bilateral/paired training with gating + checkpoint-based advancement.
results.rationale: Snippet describes bilateral/paired training combined with gating and checkpoint-based advancement — that is exactly the paired uke-tori bilateral + mastery-checkpoint-gate + staged-advancement combination claimed as the candidate's novelty. If genuine, it would be a direct twin of the recombination. Note: 2602.* arXiv ID is future-dated (Feb 2026), and I cannot verify the artefact contents from the files allowed; treating the snippet at face value gives a strong functional and semantic match.

decision_rationale: All 8 retrieved results meet the OR-rule hit threshold (every result has keyword_overlap_count >= 2 and semantic_score >= 0.7; results 3 and 8 additionally clear the 0.7 functional threshold as same-artefact + same-effect twins covering mastery-gate + advancement-criteria and bilateral-paired-curriculum-with-gating respectively). Under the lenient OR-rule, 8/8 hits indicate strong prior-art coverage of the candidate's recombination, so KATA-CHECKPOINT is not novel and the round result is FAIL.
